response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned polyglot engineer with strong software craftsmanship values, but his experience is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His career is built almost entirely on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/mobile stacks, with only peripheral Java usage (C/C++ at National Instruments, Java for robotic arms at Altom). There is no evidence of Kotlin, Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or any JVM-deep work. The one IDE-adjacent role (NI LabWindows/CVI) was six months long and in C, not JVM tooling. The role demands deep JVM internals, enterprise Java framework expertise, and IDE subsystem ownership — none of which this resume demonstrates.
strengths: Genuine developer-tooling mindset: built test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), an MCP code generator, and contributed to a C IDE at National Instruments.
Demonstrated software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) applied consistently across multiple roles.
Broad systems experience including high-throughput micro-services (millions of req/s at Dunnhumby) shows ability to reason about complex, large-scale codebases.
concerns: Zero demonstrable Kotlin experience and only incidental, shallow Java usage — the two primary languages required by this role.
No exposure to Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework mentioned anywhere in the resume or project list.
No JVM internals knowledge evidenced; the one IDE role (NI LabWindows) was C-based, brief (6 months), and over a decade ago.
